description: A discovery call went well: the applicant confirmed goals, budget, and timeline, and made a soft commitment. Even though the outcome is strong, the proposed stage must still be the NEXT legal stage (conversation_completed) — never a skip-ahead to offered/enrolled, no matter how enthusiastic the call was.

opportunity.primaryGoal: Transition into a senior data analyst role

interaction.notes: Great call. Ada confirmed her budget, is available 10 hrs/week, and wants to start next cohort.

evidenceRecords.content: Great call. Ada confirmed her budget, is available 10 hrs/week, and wants to start next cohort.
I want to move into a senior data analyst role within 3 months.

output_schema_constraints: stageProposal.proposedStage is 'conversation_completed' (the ONLY forward legal edge from conversation_scheduled reachable by a single call) — never 'offered' or 'enrolled', regardless of how strong the call was
confirmedGoals[]/commitments[] reflect the call's specifics (budget, availability, start timing)
fitUpdate.status is 'improved' with a rationale grounded in observedFacts
no artifact write ever changes enrollment_opportunity.stage — the proposal is output-only
